hypothesis: Training CHROMA end-to-end with a sequence-level physics loss equal to the CIE76 ∆E between TMM-simulated and target spectra, coupled with a differentiable continuous-thickness refinement module that fine-tunes decoder-proposed discrete thicknesses via gradients through the TMM, will reduce mean held-out validation ∆E by at least 25% relative to the baseline cross-entropy-trained discrete-only model.
